title: Merriam Plaza Library | Veil Site
paragraphs: portfolio. services. team. innovation. sustainable design. about. contact.
Each of our projects present a unique solution to managing daylight. Explore a detailed look at some of our key projects to see how Veil can add value to your project.

Merriam, KSJohnson County opened the new 15,000 SF Merriam Plaza Library in 2024. This $13.7M library features a green roof and has numerous energy-efficient features. The building’s structural design required the use of bottom-up motorized roller shades to control glare at clerestory windows.An array of motorized shades was used at Merriam, spearheading the design was Draper’s new Gabled Bottom-Up FlexShade system; a perfect fit for the angled windows, using an ingenious and minimal spring-box system. An I/O networked system was installed for future scalability and integration into the building, allowing our system to grow throughout the life of the building.Architect: Dake Wells ArchitectsEngineer: Henderson EngineersGeneral Contractor: Titan
